description: A Redis lock is the only thing preventing double-processing, so a GC pause, failover, or TTL expiry causes corruption.
body: Failure mode: a SET NX lock (or Redlock) guards a critical operation with no other protection; a process pause past the TTL, a failover that loses the key, or clock skew lets two workers run the operation concurrently and corrupt the resource. Detection signals: rare double-charges/double-processing under load or after restarts; the protected write is not idempotent; release does not check ownership. Do not treat a Redis lock as a correctness guarantee. Safer fallback: make the protected operation idempotent or use a fencing token validated at the source of truth, and release the lock only via compare-and-delete.
